subject: [PATCH] exportfs: drop unused is_export parameter from xtab_read() and xtab_write()

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the patch was not properly labeled as an nfs-utils PATCH and made a lighthearted comment about it being just cleanup
sentiment_signals: acknowledged minor issue, no clear resolution
reviews.raw_body:
Oops, I meant to make this '[nfs-utils PATCH]'. Ahh well, anyway, it's
just a bit of cleanup...

-- Jeff


On Thu, 2026-05-14 at 15:40 -0400, Jeff Layton wrote:

2026-05-14.patch_summary: This patch removes the unused 'is_export' parameter from two functions, xtab_read() and xtab_write(), in the exportfs module of the Linux kernel. The parameter was always passed as 1, making its presence unnecessary. By dropping this parameter, the code is simplified and made more efficient. This change does not affect the functionality of the exportfs module but improves its maintainability and reduces potential errors caused by incorrect usage of the 'is_export' parameter.

reviews.author: Steve Dickson
reviews.summary: Reviewer Steve Dickson noted that the patch does not include a Reviewed-by tag, which is required for kernel patches.
